proposed_relations.description: Offset bus lanes on 125th Street from east of Lenox to 2nd Avenue proposed as part of the M60 SBS project, presented to CB9 on June 5, 2013

evidence_refs.source_quote: Offset Bus Lane Eastbound and Westbound
Bus lanes from east of Lenox to 2 nd Av

rationale: Evidence explicitly shows offset bus lanes on 125th Street as part of the M60 SBS proposal in the CB9 presentation. Corridor relation already exists; adding project-level relation supported by the same evidence.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Offset Bus Lane Eastbound and Westbound
Bus lanes from east of Lenox to 2 nd Av

quarantine.note: Cited blocks p011_c0004 and p011_c0005 describe a map showing offset bus lanes and 'Bus lanes from east of Lenox to 2nd Av' as part of 'the proposed revised street design.' Neither block mentions 'M60', 'SBS', 'Select Bus Service', or any project identifier. The connection to project_m60-sbs-125th-street-2013 relies entirely on document co-occurrence (the blocks appear in a CB9 M60 SBS presentation), which the instructions explicitly state is not a relation.

Proposed crosswalk and additional pedestrian space make bus stop safer to access

rationale: Evidence explicitly presents curb extensions at Morningside Avenue as part of the M60 SBS proposal in the CB9 presentation. Morningside Avenue is west of the corridor's Amsterdam Avenue limit, so only the project relation is appropriate.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Proposed Curb Extensions at Morningside Av
Proposed crosswalk and additional pedestrian space make bus stop safer to access

quarantine.note: Cited blocks p015_c0004 ('Proposed Curb Extensions at Morningside Av') and p015_c0005 ('Proposed crosswalk and additional pedestrian space make bus stop safer to access') do not mention 'M60', 'SBS', 'Select Bus Service', or the project. proposed_relations.description: Off-board fare payment as a feature of Select Bus Service on the M60 along the 125th Street corridor

rationale: Evidence explicitly lists off-board fare payment as a core SBS feature for the M60 on 125th Street. The project relation already exists; adding the corridor-level relation since off-board fare payment serves the 125th Street corridor.

quarantine.note: The sole cited block p006_c0006 reads 'Off-board fare payment' with no mention of 125th Street, corridor, M60, or any location. This is a generic SBS feature label; relating it specifically to corridor_125th-street-manhattan is based purely on document co-occurrence, not on stated evidence in the block.

proposed_relations.description: M60 Bus Eastbound speed of 2.7 mph on 125th Street
M60 Bus Eastbound speed of 2.7 mph on 125th Street

rationale: The bar chart on page 4 explicitly labels 'M60 Bus Eastbound 2.7' as a speed measurement, naming both the M60 route and 125th Street corridor.

quarantine.note: Two relations proposed. Route relation to route_125th-laguardia-sbs is supported: 'M60 Bus Eastbound 2.7' explicitly names the M60 route — this would PASS individually. Corridor relation to corridor_125th-street-manhattan FAILS: the cited block does not state 'on 125th St' for the M60 Bus Eastbound metric. Notably, the adjacent bar in the same chart ('Driving Eastbound across 125th St') does include the corridor reference, while 'M60 Bus Eastbound' omits it. The corridor connection is inferred from chart context/co-occurrence, not stated in the block text for this metric.

proposed_relations.description: Average Walking Speed of 3.4 mph shown in the 125th Street speed comparison chart

rationale: The bar chart on page 4 compares travel modes on 125th Street; 'Average Walking Speed 3.4' appears alongside other 125th-Street-specific metrics in the comparison.

quarantine.note: Cited block p004_c0003 reads 'Average Walking Speed 3.4' — a completely generic metric with no mention of 125th Street or any corridor. 3.4 mph is a standard average human walking speed, not specific to 125th Street. The rationale that it 'appears alongside other 125th-Street-specific metrics in the comparison' is exactly co-occurrence, which the instructions explicitly state is not a relation.
